attrs.alt: Diagram for How Tool Use Works

content.text: that transforms Claude from a text generator into an action-taking system. It's a
contract between your application and the model
: you specify available operations and their input/output shapes; Claude decides when to call them and returns structured calls that your code executes.
: "Tool use is what turns Claude from a text generator into something that can take actions in the world — search the web, query a database, send an email, run a calculation."
: Claude can only generate text → "What's the weather?" → guesses from training data (wrong)
: Claude calls your function → you fetch real-time data → Claude incorporates result → accurate answer
For your distributed systems work at Box Hill
Call APIs, run code, query DB
Use direct response for trivial tasks

1. You define tools (name + description + JSON Schema input_schema)
2. You call Claude with tools=... and tool_choice
3. Claude either responds with text OR asks to call tools (stop_reason == "tool_use")
4. Your app executes tool(s) and sends results back as tool_result blocks (references tool_use_id)
5. Repeat until stop_reason == "end_turn"

Your app: "Here are available tools and their shapes"
Claude: "I need to use tool X with inputs Y"
Your app: "Okay, executing tool X with Y → result Z"
Claude: "Got result Z → now I can continue reasoning"

Step 1: You define tool
  └─ name: "get_stock_price"
  └─ description: "Get current stock price for symbol"
  └─ input_schema: { symbol: "string", required: ["symbol"] }

Step 2: Claude returns tool_use block
  └─ type: "tool_use"
  └─ id: "toolu_01A12v3xX"
  └─ name: "get_stock_price"
  └─ input: { symbol: "AAPL" }

Step 3: Your app sends tool_result
  └─ type: "tool_result"
  └─ tool_use_id: "toolu_01A12v3xX"
  └─ content: "175.43"
: Description is what Claude reads to decide whether to call the tool.
: "Check stock" → vague, Claude won't use it
: "Get current stock price for symbol. Use when you need real-time stock prices." → specific, Claude calls it
array contains both text + tool_use blocks
must match the original tool_use block's
acts as control flow signal for your agent.
, Claude calls tool when request maps to tool's capability and answer isn't in context.
is your primary indicator for agent logic.
Step 4: Execute Tool in Your Code
Step 5: Send Tool Result Back to Claude
: Claude can call 2+ tools in parallel, efficiency gain.
: Client tools = you handle execution; Server tools = Anthropic handles execution.
: Shrinks code to "describe tool → send prompt → wait for result".
: Claude can still use tools from
: Claude must use at least one tool
: "What are the stock prices for AAPL and GOOGL?"
Tool use = contract between app and model
→ You define tools, Claude decides when to call
: Define → Tool Use → Tool Result → Repeat
→ Vague = bad tool use, specific = good
For your Box Hill distributed systems work, I'd implement
the three-step handshake with parallel tool calls + Tool Runner SDK
, giving you automatic schema generation, reduced turns, and efficiency gains while maintaining full control over tool execution.
